reasoning: The ECB is engaging in Quantitative Tightening (QT), actively shrinking its balance sheet by allowing its APP and PEPP portfolios to run off without reinvestment. As a result, the dominant trend for the central bank assets (ECBASSETSW) is definitively downward. Over short horizons, such as one week (2025-11-02) or one month (2025-11-25), the balance sheet is highly likely to shrink because QT operates on a steady schedule of bond maturities (which often occur at month-end). Weekly volatility from Main Refinancing Operations could introduce some noise, but it rarely overshadows the structural decline during normal periods. For the longest horizon (2026-01-24), there is more epistemic uncertainty. The end-of-year 2025 Eurosystem balance sheet has some revaluation effects and potential liquidity adjustments, and macroeconomic conditions might prompt the ECB to alter its policy path; therefore, the probability of an increase is somewhat higher due to wider uncertainty. As instructed, the probabilities move closer to 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends to reflect this growing uncertainty.
